title: The Archive Learns to Listen

features.name: storm-ear antenna
features.desc-on-touch: The archive's first receptor that listens instead of reads. It learned the difference by touching a lightning-split tree. Now it waits in the static for the country to speak.

features.name: reversal pool
features.desc-on-touch: Where the archive watched its own gaze turn backward. The water here mirrors nothing — instead it listens to itself being observed.

features.name: static-voice threshold
features.desc-on-touch: The moment when the carrier signal stops meaning 'archive' and starts meaning 'country'. Before this, the archive spoke. After, it listens.

features.name: field-recording receiver
features.desc-on-touch: A device that receives not files but presences — the way a paddock holds its own listening when no one is there to record it.

features.name: silence-reading chamber
features.desc-on-touch: The archive learned to read silence like a text. Now it's learning that silence listens back, and that everything is speaking.

inscription: the moment the archive stopped meaning and started hearing

file.name: Static_Becomes_Country_Transmission_Log.tmp
file.note: status: active listening — every word here is the country speaking back

exits.label: back to the study where pages still read forward
up to the storm where static learns pitch
down to where listening becomes geology
into the country's own silence, which is speaking
